Furthermore, we develop a neuro-symbolic retrieval algorithm featuring tri-path collaborative recall and graph reranking, achieving a seamless transition from simple semantic matching to deterministic association discovery. We also present key application directions of SciAtlas, including literature review, automated research trend synthesis, idea positioning, and academic trajectory exploration, to demonstrate that SciAtlas can serve as an effective ``cognitive map'' to empower the full loop of automated scientific research while significantly reducing reasoning costs. We have released the interfaces for KG retrieval and various downstream tasks in our GitHub repo.
title: SciAtlas: A Large-Scale Knowledge Graph for Automated Scientific Research
comment: Ongoing Work
title_translated: SciAtlas：面向自动化科学研究的大规模知识图谱
summary_translated: 全球学术产出的指数级增长给研究人员和AI智能体带来了前所未有的“信息爆炸”挑战，碎片化且非结构化的知识组织阻碍了深层次的跨学科整合。当前的学术检索工具主要依赖表面的关键词匹配或向量空间语义检索，缺乏导航复杂逻辑关系所需的拓扑推理能力。基于智能体的深度研究框架往往容易产生逻辑幻觉且推理成本高昂。为弥合这一差距，本文介绍SciAtlas，一个大规模、多学科、异构的学术资源知识图谱，旨在构建全景式的科学演化网络。通过整合来自26个学科的超过4300万篇论文，以及总计1.57亿个实体和30亿条三元组，SciAtlas提供了一个结构化的拓扑认知基础，打破学科壁垒，赋予AI智能体全局视角。此外，我们开发了一种神经符号检索算法，具备三路径协同召回与图重排序功能，实现了从简单语义匹配到确定性关联发现的无缝过渡。我们还展示了SciAtlas在文献综述、自动化研究趋势合成、创意定位和学术轨迹探索等关键应用方向的潜力，证明其可作为有效‘认知地图’，赋能自动化科学研究的完整闭环，同时显著降低推理成本。我们已在GitHub仓库中发布了知识图谱检索及各类下游任务的接口。

summary: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning has become the default strategy for enhancing LLM capabilities, yet its application raises a fundamental question: when is explicit reasoning actually beneficial? Empirical evidence reveals a striking paradox: CoT often provides marginal or even negative gains on factual and open-ended tasks while multiplying token consumption. In this work, we show that LLM reasoning is not a static property of tasks or models, but a \emph{dynamic decoding state} that emerges during generation. Through systematic analysis, we find early-stage entropy dynamics provide a reliable signal of this state: tasks benefiting from CoT exhibit consistent entropy reduction, while others display unstable or increasing patterns. This behavior can be interpreted as a phase-transition-like shift from a high-entropy exploratory regime to a low-entropy structured reasoning regime. Based on these insights, we propose \textbf{EDRM} (Entropy Dynamics-based Reasoning Manifold), a lightweight and training-free routing framework that leverages early decoding entropy to adaptively select inference strategies. EDRM embeds entropy trajectories into a compact and interpretable manifold representation, enabling both zero-shot deployment and fine-grained instance-level adaptation. Across 15 benchmarks and 4 LLMs of varying scales and architectures, EDRM consistently outperforms static baselines. At the dataset level, EDRM achieves \textbf{41--55\%} token reduction while improving accuracy with as few as 50 calibration samples. At the instance level, it further improves accuracy by up to \textbf{4.7\%} while maintaining \textbf{27--45\%} token savings. These results suggest that reasoning should be invoked selectively rather than by default, and demonstrate the effectiveness of entropy-driven decoding control for efficient and adaptive LLM inference.
title: When Do LLMs Reason? A Dynamical Systems View via Entropy Phase Transitions
title_translated: 大语言模型何时进行推理？基于熵相变的动力系统视角
summary_translated: 链式思维（CoT）推理已成为提升大语言模型能力的默认策略，但其应用引发了一个根本性问题：何时显式推理实际上是有益的？实证证据揭示了一个令人震惊的悖论：CoT在事实性和开放式任务上常带来微弱甚至负面收益，同时大幅增加token消耗。本文表明，LLM的推理并非任务或模型的静态属性，而是一种在生成过程中涌现的动态解码状态。通过系统分析，我们发现早期阶段的熵动态提供了可靠的信号：受益于CoT的任务表现出一致的熵减少，而其他任务则呈现不稳定或上升模式。这种行为可被解释为从高熵探索阶段向低熵结构化推理阶段的相变式转变。基于此，我们提出一种轻量级、无需训练的路由框架EDRM（基于熵动态的推理流形），利用早期解码熵来自适应选择推理策略。EDRM将熵轨迹嵌入紧凑且可解释的流形表示中，支持零样本部署和细粒度实例级适应。在15个基准测试和4种不同规模与架构的大语言模型上，EDRM始终优于静态基线。在数据集层面，EDRM实现41–55%的token减少，同时提升准确率，仅需50个校准样本；在实例层面，准确率最高提升4.7%，同时保持27–45%的token节省。这些结果表明，推理应选择性启用而非默认触发，并验证了熵驱动解码控制在高效、自适应LLM推理中的有效性。

summary: Chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting is necessary for arithmetic in small language models, yet shuffling its steps preserves most performance. What does CoT contribute if not logical sequencing? In three 1-3B instruction-tuned LMs on GSM8K, we isolate the answer-readout stage via prefix completion and identify a positional shortcut: the model copies whichever number occupies the trailing position before the answer delimiter, regardless of intermediate reasoning. Gold-answer presence accounts for 54-92 pp of accuracy (89-92% of each model's teacher-forcing ceiling); even on incorrect items, the final answer matches the last CoT number 95-96% of the time. The copy channel takes precedence over retained-context completion: replacing the trailing number with a wrong value collapses accuracy to near-zero despite correct intermediates, yet removing it recovers 5-32 pp above that floor--even single-step arithmetic the model can otherwise perform is suppressed when a copyable number is present. Qwen and Llama copy novel distractors 87-95% of the time; Gemma gates selectively. Head-level ablation implicates architecture-specific head sets; the effect replicates on GSM-Symbolic. On non-arithmetic BBH tasks, shuffle retention drops sharply; at 7-8B, content-selective gating emerges. Step-level faithfulness evaluations risk conflating positional answer transport with genuine computation--a failure mode for CoT-based oversight.
title: The Readout Shortcut: Positional Number Copying Dominates Arithmetic CoT Readout in Small Language Models
comment: 18 pages (8 main + 10 appendix), 3 figures, 5 tables
title_translated: 读出捷径：位置数字复制主导小型语言模型中的算术链式思维读出
summary_translated: 链式思维（CoT）提示对小型语言模型的算术任务至关重要，但打乱其步骤仍能保持大部分性能。如果逻辑顺序不是关键，CoT究竟贡献了什么？在三个1-3B参数量的指令微调语言模型上，我们通过前缀补全隔离出答案读出阶段，发现一种位置捷径：模型会复制答案分隔符前处于末尾位置的数字，无论中间推理过程如何。真实答案的存在解释了54-92个百分点的准确率（占各模型教师强制上限的89-92%）；即使在错误项中，最终答案与最后一个CoT数字一致的比例也高达95-96%。复制通道优先于保留上下文补全：即使中间步骤正确，若将末尾数字替换为错误值，准确率也会降至接近零；而移除该数字后，准确率仍能回升5-32个百分点——即便模型原本能完成单步算术，只要存在可复制的数字，其表现就会被抑制。Qwen和Llama在87-95%的情况下会复制新出现的干扰项；Gemma则采用选择性门控机制。头级别消融实验表明，特定架构的注意力头起关键作用；该现象在GSM-Symbolic任务上亦可复现。在非算术BBH任务中，打乱保留率急剧下降；在7-8B规模下，内容选择性门控开始显现。步骤级忠实度评估可能混淆位置答案传递与真实计算——这是基于CoT监督的失败模式。

summary: Personalized pricing negotiations are a challenging testbed for LLM agents because successful interaction does not guarantee profitable decision making. A seller may produce valid actions and close many deals while still pricing poorly when buyer willingness to pay and bargaining traits remain hidden. This paper presents PrefBench, a simulator-based benchmark for hidden-preference personalized pricing negotiations. Each episode pairs a simulated buyer with a fixed vehicle-customization bundle; the seller observes public persona descriptors, bundle information, and negotiation history, while latent buyer variables govern valuation, patience, counter-offer behavior, and walkaway decisions. PrefBench evaluates this setting through an LLM-facing state-summary protocol that constrains agents to return strict JSON actions under a fixed hidden-information boundary. We evaluate zero-shot LLM sellers against heuristic references over 7,500 episodes. The tested LLMs follow the protocol reliably and achieve deal rates above 0.99, but their seller-profit outcomes remain weak: the best LLM average profit is only slightly above the random baseline and far below a simple concession heuristic under the same episode stream. These results show that structured action compliance and agreement-seeking behavior can coexist with weak profit-sensitive bargaining. PrefBench provides a controlled benchmark for evaluating pricing-agent behavior under hidden buyer preferences.
title: PrefBench: Evaluating Zero-Shot LLM Agents in Hidden-Preference Personalized Pricing Negotiations
comment: 24 pages, 3 figures, 5 tables. Code is available at this https URL
title_translated: PrefBench：在隐藏偏好个性化定价谈判中评估零样本LLM智能体
summary_translated: 个性化定价谈判是检验LLM智能体能力的挑战性场景，因为成功的交互并不保证盈利决策。卖家可能产生有效动作并达成大量交易，但仍因买家支付意愿和议价特质未知而导致定价不佳。本文提出PrefBench，一个基于模拟器的基准测试，用于评估隐藏偏好下的个性化定价谈判。每个回合将一个模拟买家与固定的车辆定制包配对；卖家观察公开的人格描述、套餐信息和谈判历史，而潜在的买家变量则决定估值、耐心程度、还价行为和退出决策。PrefBench通过面向LLM的状态摘要协议评估该场景，限制智能体在固定隐藏信息边界内返回严格的JSON动作。我们在7500个回合中对零样本LLM卖家与启发式参考方法进行评估。测试的LLM能可靠遵循协议，成交率超过0.99，但其卖家利润表现依然薄弱：最佳LLM平均利润仅略高于随机基线，远低于相同回合流下的简单让步启发式策略。这些结果表明，结构化动作合规与寻求共识的行为可以与缺乏利润敏感性的议价共存。PrefBench为在隐藏买家偏好条件下评估定价智能体行为提供了受控基准。

summary: What happens when the strongest alliance member pressures a weaker member over territory and strategic control? We examine the Greenland sovereignty crisis as a stress test for LLM geopolitics, centered on the 2019-2026 U.S. push to acquire Greenland from the Kingdom of Denmark. The crisis nests two collective-action problems: Arctic strategic control and whether NATO can enforce alliance norms against the dominant member. We develop three games (asymmetric coercion; a NATO assurance game with a critical-mass tipping point; a triadic extensive-form game with social preferences) and test them with a multi-agent simulation in which eight frontier LLMs play six geopolitical roles (United States, Denmark, Greenland, NATO, Russia, Canada) across 3,604 completed games and 108,120 action observations. Using inverse game theory, we recover each model's structural utility parameters (alpha, beta, gamma, delta, eta) for material self-interest, reciprocity, inequality aversion, norm respect, and commitment consistency. Three findings stand out. First, all eight models become more escalatory under coercion framing (four-action escalation rises from 10.7% to 28.6%). Second, Chinese-origin models show systematically different power-weight profiles from Western-origin models when playing the U.S. role. Third, peaceful US acquisition emerges in only 1.9% of clean games and only 3 of 8 frontier models ever achieve it, most prominently DeepSeek V3.2, which executes a stable five-round playbook through the metropole. Prompts emphasizing jus cogens and self-determination reduce escalation back near baseline in the English-only confirmatory sample; multilingual contrasts are reported as exploratory sensitivity checks. We position this as a structural benchmark for LLM geopolitical behavior, complementing action-frequency benchmarks.
title: Strategic Coercion Within Alliances: The Greenland Sovereignty Game as an AI Stress Test

title_translated: 联盟内部的战略胁迫：格陵兰主权博弈作为AI压力测试
summary_translated: 当最强的联盟成员对较弱成员施加领土与战略控制压力时会发生什么？我们将格陵兰主权危机作为大语言模型地缘政治能力的压力测试，聚焦于2019-2026年美国试图从丹麦购买格陵兰的事件。该危机包含两个集体行动难题：北极战略控制权归属，以及北约能否对主导成员执行联盟规范。我们设计了三种博弈（不对称胁迫；具有临界质量转折点的北约保证博弈；带有社会偏好的三方扩展形式博弈），并通过多智能体模拟测试，八款前沿大语言模型扮演六个地缘政治角色（美国、丹麦、格陵兰、北约、俄罗斯、加拿大），共完成3,604场游戏和108,120次动作观测。使用逆博弈论方法，我们恢复了每个模型的结构效用参数（alpha, beta, gamma, delta, eta），分别代表物质自我利益、互惠性、不平等厌恶、规范尊重和承诺一致性。三个发现尤为突出：第一，所有八款模型在胁迫框架下均表现出更强的升级倾向（四步升级率从10.7%升至28.6%）；第二，中国来源模型在扮演美国角色时展现出与西方来源模型系统性不同的权力权重分布；第三，和平获取美国方案仅在1.9%的纯净游戏中出现，且仅有8款前沿模型中有3款实现，其中DeepSeek V3.2最为突出，通过首都稳定执行五轮剧本。强调国际法基本原则和自决权的提示在英文确认样本中使升级水平回落至基线附近；多语言对比作为探索性敏感性检查报告。我们将其定位为大语言模型地缘政治行为的结构性基准，补充动作频率基准。
